ingredients.item: boneless, skinless chicken breasts, patted dry
extra-virgin olive oil, plus more as needed

steps.text: Pat the chicken dry. Season well with salt and pepper (if you’re using chicken breasts, cut them into cutlets for even cooking).
Dust the chicken lightly with flour and shake off the excess.
Heat olive oil in a large pan; add butter. Sear the chicken over medium-high heat until golden, then flip and cook until browned and cooked through. Remove to a plate.
Lower the heat and cook garlic until fragrant. Stir in tomato paste until it darkens slightly, then add oregano and red-pepper flakes.
Pour in chicken stock and simmer, scraping up browned bits, until the liquid reduces by about half.
Stir in the cream and warm until the sauce thickens. Add grated Parmesan and sun-dried tomatoes; adjust seasoning as needed.
Return the chicken to the pan just to warm through. Finish with fresh basil and serve.
